FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class ReactiveRuntime

Namespace
FluentGpu.Signals
Assembly
FluentGpu.Foundation.dll

The scheduler: owns the pending-effect queue, the batch depth, and the "a frame is needed" callback the host wires to wake its loop. One per AppHost (so headless tests don't cross-contaminate). Single-threaded.

public sealed class ReactiveRuntime
Inheritance
object
ReactiveRuntime

object.GetHashCode()
object.GetType()
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.ToString()

Fields

FrameRequested

Set by the host: called (once-ish) when work becomes pending, so the host schedules a frame.

public Action FrameRequested

Field Value

Action

Properties

HasPending

True when effects are queued and waiting for the next Flush().

public bool HasPending { get; }

Property Value

bool

Methods

Batch(Action)

Coalesce many signal writes (e.g. a pointer-drag burst) into one flush.

public void Batch(Action action)

Parameters

action Action

Flush()

Drain all pending effects (and any they transitively schedule) — called by the host once per frame.

public void Flush()
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
